title: centaurea × intermedia
intro: Centaurea × intermedia, also known as cornflower, is a hybrid plant that belongs to the asteraceae family and is a cross between Centaurea cyanus and Centaurea montana. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Centaurea × intermedia include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and act as an antiseptic, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for skin and digestive health. Herbal preparations of Centaurea × intermedia include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
